title: galium album subsp. prusense
intro: Galium album subsp. prusense, also known as Prussian winterberry or white bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium album plant, a flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Galium album subsp. prusense include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. Galium album subsp. prusense can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
